# AgentSite Kit — Full Technical Reference

> Make any website Agent-friendly. Scan, classify, extract, and serve structured data for AI agents.

Version: 1.0.2
License: MIT
Repository: https://github.com/lingfan36/AgentSite-Kit
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/agentsite-kit

---

## Architecture

```
src/
├── commands/       # CLI commands (init, scan, generate, serve, update, mcp)
├── config/         # Configuration loading, validation, persistence
├── scanner/        # Website crawler, content extractor, page classifier
├── generator/      # Output file generators (llms.txt, JSON, etc.)
├── server/         # Fastify API server, routes, dashboard
│   └── routes/     # content-routes, search, site-routes
├── mcp/            # MCP (Model Context Protocol) server
├── llm/            # LLM integration for classification/summarization
├── plugins/        # Plugin system with lifecycle hooks
├── types/          # TypeScript type definitions
├── utils/          # Shared utilities (search, hash, access-control, etc.)
└── web/            # Dashboard HTML
```

## CLI Commands

### agentsite init
Create `agentsite.config.yaml` interactively.
Options: `-t <template>` (use industry template), `--list-templates` (list templates)

Templates: docs-site, blog, saas, knowledge-base, ecommerce, portfolio, api-docs, community

### agentsite scan
Scan website and classify pages.
Options: `--no-llm` (disable LLM-assisted classification)

Process:
1. Fetch sitemap (if available)
2. Crawl pages (respects maxPages, concurrency, delayMs, robots.txt)
3. Extract content (title, headings, body, metadata)
4. Classify pages (rule-based or LLM-assisted)
5. Generate summaries (rule-based or LLM-assisted)
6. Write scan-result.json

### agentsite generate
Generate Agent-friendly files from scan results.

Output files:
- llms.txt — LLM-friendly plain text overview
- agent-sitemap.json — Machine-readable sitemap with metadata
- agent-index.json — Structured site index with API endpoint directory
- data/docs.json — Documentation entries
- data/faq.json — FAQ entries
- data/products.json — Product entries
- data/articles.json — Blog/article entries
- data/pricing.json — Pricing/plan entries
- data/changelog.json — Changelog/release entries

### agentsite serve
Start the Fastify API server.
Options: `-p, --port <port>` (default: 3141)

### agentsite update
Incremental update — re-scan, detect changes, regenerate only what changed.

### agentsite mcp
Start MCP server over stdio for direct AI tool integration.

---

## Configuration Schema

File: `agentsite.config.yaml`

```yaml
site:
  url: string          # Required. Website URL to scan
  name: string         # Required. Site name
  description: string  # Site description (default: "")

scan:
  maxPages: number     # Max pages to crawl (default: 100)
  concurrency: number  # Concurrent requests (default: 3)
  delayMs: number      # Delay between requests in ms (default: 200)
  include: string[]    # Glob patterns to include (default: ["**"])
  exclude: string[]    # Glob patterns to exclude (default: [])
  respectRobotsTxt: boolean  # (default: true)

output:
  dir: string          # Output directory (default: ".agentsite")
  formats: string[]    # Output formats: llms-txt, agent-sitemap, agent-index, structured

server:
  port: number         # Server port (default: 3141)
  rateLimit:
    max: number        # Max requests per window (default: 60)
    timeWindow: string # Rate limit window (default: "1 minute")
  accessLog: boolean   # Enable access logging (default: true)

access:
  allowedPages: string[]   # Glob patterns for allowed pages (default: ["**"])
  blockedPages: string[]   # Glob patterns for blocked pages (default: [])
  allowedTypes: string[]   # Allowed page types (default: [docs, faq, blog, product, pricing, about, contact, changelog])
  summaryOnly: boolean     # Only expose summaries, not full content (default: false)
  allowSearch: boolean     # Enable search endpoint (default: true)

llm:                   # Optional — enables AI-assisted classification/summarization
  apiUrl: string       # OpenAI-compatible API URL (default: "https://api.openai.com/v1")
  apiKey: string       # API key
  model: string        # Model name (default: "gpt-4o-mini")

plugins: string[]      # Plugin file paths (default: [])

sites:                 # Optional — multi-site mode
  - site: { url, name, description }
    scan: { ... }
    output: { ... }
    access: { ... }
```

---

## API Reference

Base URL: `http://localhost:3141`

### Content Endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /api/health | Health check. Returns `{ ok: true }` |
| GET | /api/search?q={query} | Full-text search across all pages |
| GET | /api/pages/{id} | Get single page by URL-derived ID |
| GET | /api/docs?section={section} | Documentation entries, optional section filter |
| GET | /api/faq?category={category} | FAQ entries, optional category filter |
| GET | /api/products | Product entries |
| GET | /api/articles?tag={tag}&page={n}&limit={n} | Articles with pagination and tag filter |
| GET | /api/pricing | Pricing/plan entries |
| GET | /api/changelog | Changelog entries |
| GET | /api/stats | Site statistics (page counts, word counts, type breakdown) |
| GET | /api/config | Current configuration (API keys masked) |
| GET | /api/files | List generated files with content |
| GET | /api/access-log?limit={n} | Recent access log entries |
| GET | /api/pages-data | All pages with metadata |

### Site Management Endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /api/sites | List all configured sites |
| POST | /api/sites | Add a new site. Body: `{ url, name, description }` |
| DELETE | /api/sites/{slug} | Remove a site by slug |
| POST | /api/sites/{slug}/scan | Trigger scan + generate for a specific site |

### Operation Endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | /api/rescan | Rescan all sites. Optional body: `{ url }` for ad-hoc scan |
| POST | /api/regenerate | Regenerate output files from existing scan data |
| POST | /api/webhook | Webhook trigger (requires AGENTSITE_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var). Body: `{ secret, urls? }` |

### Static File Routes

| Path | Description |
|------|-------------|
| GET /llms.txt | LLM-friendly site overview |
| GET /agent-sitemap.json | Machine-readable sitemap |
| GET /agent-index.json | Structured site index |
| GET /.well-known/ai-plugin.json | AI plugin manifest |

### Multi-Site Endpoints

When multiple sites are configured, each site gets prefixed endpoints:
- GET /api/{slug}/pages-data
- GET /api/{slug}/search?q={query}
- GET /api/{slug}/docs
- GET /api/{slug}/faq
- GET /api/{slug}/products
- GET /api/{slug}/articles
- GET /api/{slug}/pricing
- GET /api/{slug}/changelog

---

## Type Definitions

### PageType
```
'homepage' | 'docs' | 'faq' | 'blog' | 'product' | 'pricing' | 'about' | 'contact' | 'changelog' | 'unknown'
```

### ScannedPage
```
{
  url: string
  title: string
  type: PageType
  contentHash: string
  summary: string
  headings: string[]
  lastModified?: string
  scannedAt: string
  wordCount: number
  tags?: string[]
  version?: string
  author?: string
  publishedAt?: string
  updatedAt?: string
  isSpa?: boolean
}
```

### Structured Data Types

DocEntry: { title, url, section, summary, tags, updated_at }
FaqEntry: { question, answer, category, url, updated_at }
ProductEntry: { product_name, description, features, pricing, url, updated_at }
ArticleEntry: { title, summary, published_at, updated_at, tags, url }
PricingEntry: { plan_name, price, features, url, updated_at }
ChangelogEntry: { version, date, changes, url }

---

## MCP Tools

| Tool | Description | Parameters |
|------|-------------|------------|
| search | Search site content | query: string, limit?: number |
| get_page | Get page by URL | url: string |
| list_pages | List pages | type?: PageType |
| list_faq | List FAQs | category?: string |
| list_docs | List docs | section?: string |
| list_products | List products | (none) |
| list_articles | List articles | tag?: string |
| list_pricing | List pricing | (none) |
| list_changelog | List changelog | version?: string |
| site_overview | Site stats | (none) |
| get_config | Config summary | (none) |
| ask | Natural language Q&A | question: string |

## MCP Resources

| Resource | URI | Description |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| llms-txt | file:///llms.txt | LLM-friendly site overview |
| agent-index | file:///agent-index.json | Site metadata and API endpoints |
| agent-sitemap | file:///agent-sitemap.json | Page metadata sitemap |
| data-{type} | file:///data/{type}.json | Structured data (docs, faq, products, articles, pricing, changelog) |

---

## Plugin System

Plugins are JavaScript modules that export lifecycle hooks:

```javascript
export default {
  name: 'my-plugin',
  beforeScan(config) { },
  afterScan(pages) { return pages; },
  beforeGenerate(scanResult) { },
  afterGenerate(outDir) { },
}
```

Hooks: beforeScan, afterScan, beforeGenerate, afterGenerate

---

## Docker Deployment

```bash
docker compose up -d
# or
docker build -t agentsite .
docker run -p 3141:3141 -v ./agentsite.config.yaml:/app/agentsite.config.yaml:ro agentsite
```

## MCP Client Configuration

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "my-site": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["agentsite-kit", "mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```
